violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Staff are enrolled in class. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Staff informed inspector that hair nets have been ordered. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

The concentration of the sanitizer solution for the wet wiping cloths was 0 parts per million (ppm). The required concentration for quaternary ammonium sanitizer is 200-400 ppm. Staff emptied and refilled sanitizer to proper concentration. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026
Refrigerator door gaskets of the open prep unit were observed in poor repair. PIC stated gaskets have been ordered. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2026
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Two staff members are presently registered for class. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

Per interview of PIC, blenders and utensils are only washed, rinsed, and sanitized at the end of the day. The surfaces of equipment that contacts time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as meats and cheeses and dairy must be washed, rinsed, and sanitized at least every four hours. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Ice disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026
A container of hummus was observed with date mark exceeding 7 days. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Establishment is heat-sealing banana cream parfaits without approval. Establishment to cease practice until detailed process and request is submitted and approved. Items were removed from sale, PIC to return to main commissary. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Yogurt and banana parfaits packaged in the food establishment's commissary kitchen are lacking complete labels. Sub ingredients of yogurt (yogurt parfait) and grahams (banana parfait), common allergens were missing. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024

The concentration of the sanitizer solution for the wet wiping cloths was 0 parts per million (ppm). The required concentration for quaternary ammonium sanitizer is 200-300 ppm. Sani buckets were refilled to proper concentration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026
Refrigerator door gaskets of the open air prep unit were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2026

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sinks and sani buckets. Inspector provided during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026
The floor drain under the dish machine was observed to be clogged, with water backing-up when machine is running. PIC called for service. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety licensed with RIDOH. PIC stated manager is in process of completing course. Inspector provided application. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the service area. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. PIC obtained soap during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Yogurt parfaits packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels; overnight oats were observed with incomplete labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. PIC took parfaits from self-service case to behind the counter. Inspector provided guidance on labeling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. PIC gave staff hairnets to wear. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024
Dessert prep unit located in the service area was not in good repair. Inspector observed a loose handle during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -stand mixer -prep unit gaskets (schedules for replacement today). New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024
A leak was observed at the grease trap. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety licensed with RIDOH. Inspector provided application. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024
Employees were observed donning gloves without washing their hands. Food employees must wash their hands before putting on gloves. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

Raw animal foods (liquid eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (milk, vegetables) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized; ice disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

Milk was observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed milk containers in refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

Multiple containers in the walk-in refrigerator were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024

Frozen sauce was observed thawing in standing water in the 3-bay sink. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

Various containers of food were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in refrigerator and freezer, on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice bin) between uses. Knives were observed stored between a prep unit and table. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024
Cases of single service items are being stored on the floor in the storage areas. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024

The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. Inspector provided during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -ice bin (COS) -gaskets of prep units -stand mixer. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024
A leak was observed at the grease trap. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Inspector discussed alternative methods for rinsing produce until an air gap can be installed. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024
